method_atom: A duality that assigns thermodynamic state variables (temperature and entropy) to a boundary separating accessible from inaccessible regions of a system, deriving that temperature from analytic-continuation / path-integral (imaginary-time periodicity) arguments and identifying the entropy with a conserved current (Noether) charge of the underlying symmetry. The governing field equations of the theory are then recast as, and alternatively re-derived from, a thermodynamic relation — in particular by extremizing (maximizing) an entropy functional, so that the dynamical laws emerge as an equilibrium/extremum condition rather than being postulated.
object_atom: The gravitational field and spacetime geometry — specifically causal/bifurcation horizons (including black-hole horizons and their evaporation) in general diffeomorphism-invariant theories of gravity such as Lanczos-Lovelock models, and the field equations governing that geometry.
brief_reason: What carries across is the boundary-thermodynamics-plus-entropy-extremization machinery (temperature/entropy assigned to an information boundary, dynamics recovered from maximizing entropy); what is domain-specific is spacetime horizons and gravitational field equations, though the horizon concept is somewhat fused with gravity here.
